title: Please, Check!
description: Play a polite quantum fact-checker who swipes between color lanes and taps unstable gates to verify the right results before bad physics gets through.

howToPlayJson.title: Please, Check!
howToPlayJson.objective: Survive a 2-4 minute review run by matching your cursor to incoming gate colors, using timed checks on uncertain gates, and building a combo multiplier through accurate approvals.

controls.outcome: Move the review cursor to another colored lane before the next gate arrives.

controls.input: Tap an incoming gate marked with a ?
controls.outcome: Reveal its true color for a moment so you can decide whether to stay, swap, or prepare a combo save.

controls.input: Press and hold the PLEASE button when charged
controls.outcome: Briefly slows the stream and shows the next two gates, but drains the charge immediately.

steps: Start in the middle lane with a glowing review cursor.
Colored gates stream downward; passing through a matching gate scores points and adds combo.
A mismatched gate breaks combo and costs one heart.
Some gates arrive as uncertain ? gates; tap them before contact to collapse them into a real color.
Every few seconds a PLEASE request appears and charges the PLEASE button if you clear it cleanly.
Using PLEASE briefly slows time and previews the next pair of gates, letting you plan around dense patterns.
Later waves introduce entangled gate pairs: checking one instantly changes the color of another lane's next gate, forcing quick reroutes.
The run ends when all hearts are lost, then the score posts to a simple local leaderboard.
howToPlayJson.scoring: Score 10 per matched gate, +5 per successful check, and a multiplier that rises every 5 clean matches up to x6. Missing a check on a ? gate or hitting a wrong color resets the multiplier. Entangled pair clears give a 2x,
tips: Save PLEASE for entangled waves, not single uncertain gates.
Tap ? gates early; the reveal window is short.
A high combo matters more than chasing every risky bonus check.
Audio pitch rises with multiplier, so use sound to track momentum without looking away from the lanes.
